name: Danny Barker
abstract: Daniel Moses Barker (January 13, 1909 – March 13, 1994) was an American jazz musician, vocalist, and author from New Orleans. He was a rhythm guitarist for Cab Calloway, Lucky Millinder and Benny Carter during the 1930s.

interviews.title: Danny Barker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

concerts.label: Folk Masters: Crescent City Strut: New Orleans Jazz from Second-Line to Dance Hall

works_created.t: Save the Bones for Henry Jones

about_quotes.speaker: David Torkanowsky
about_quotes.evidence: when I came out as a kid it was like man, I was playing with Danny Barker on Bourbon Street for six hours then I’d go play a bebop gig with James Black and Earl Turpentine after.
about_quotes.doc_title: David Torkanowsky interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, New York, September 26, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Al Rose
about_quotes.evidence: the first session that I made under my own aegis... was a record of Dan Burley called Chicken Shack Shuffled, 78, with, uh, Pop Sposter and Danny Barker and Jimmy Archie.

about_quotes.speaker: Louis Barbarin
about_quotes.evidence: In 1947, I went to New York with Danny Barker, speculating on his job. And I got the USO group. Was going to Japan. So I made the trip. Played Manila Okinawa in Japan.

about_quotes.speaker: Jo Jones
about_quotes.evidence: Now whose on the date? Teddy Wilson and his orchestra. But look who's on the date? ... Danny Barker on bass. Milt Hinton, Cozy Cole, and Billie Holiday.

about_quotes.speaker: Albert Burbank
about_quotes.evidence: I believe Danny Barker played about his first job with me... I had a three piece job... We was working on a thing every night.

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Dutrey
about_quotes.evidence: I started working with them, it was Lee Collins, Fess Jones, myself, Daniel Barker, Roy Evans, and Joe Rovershue.
